choices.text: Auto-routes review requests based on file paths in the PR. `.github/CODEOWNERS` maps paths to teams/users; when a PR touches files matching a pattern, GitHub auto-requests reviews from the listed owners. Pair with branch protection's 'Require review from CODEOWNERS' to make these blocking. Doesn't grant any access — owners still need normal repo permissions via team membership. The point is routing: payments PRs go to payments team, security PRs go to security team, no manual assignment needed. Use @org/team-name for resilience (people come and go; team membership updates centrally).

questions.explanation: Auto-routes review requests by file path. Doesn't grant access; complements branch protection.

questions.prompt: DevOps team is CODEOWNER for package.json. Dependabot opens 20 PRs/week, all auto-routing to DevOps. Mitigation?

choices.text: Enable auto-merge for bot PRs that pass CI. Configure Dependabot (or Renovate) with auto-merge: minor and patch updates that pass all CI checks merge automatically without human review. Reserve human review for major version bumps (which actually require attention). Combine with a strict CI suite — if tests pass, the update is safe. Optionally: add a 'dependabot' team to spread CODEOWNERS load across multiple people instead of one team. The 20 PRs/week problem becomes manageable; humans review only the 2-3 majors per week that genuinely warrant attention.

questions.explanation: Auto-merge bot PRs that pass CI; reserve human review for major bumps that actually need it.
